chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: 2719 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِنْ أَلْقَى اَلْمُحْرِمُ اَلْقُرَادَ عَنْ بَعِيرِهِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ وَ لاَ يُلْقِي اَلْحَلَمَةَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2719 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If a person in the state of ihram removes ticks from their camel, there is no harm in that, but they should not remove lice."
